Izabel rarely reads from a script.
Morning, Izabel. We are all here. Whenever you are ready, go ahead and open the call.
Good morning, everyone. As I usually do, I will start with the cash flow and then move to operations.
Sounds good. Before you do, could you remind the new partners what you oversee here?
Of course. I run operations at AVRT and I lead a small team. I track the key figures every week and liaise with our suppliers.
Great. One of the new partners is asking about the ERP timeline. Do you have a date?
That is a good question. I do not have the exact date in front of me, so let me check that and get back to you today.
Perfect. That is exactly the right answer. Please go ahead with the cash flow.
Thank you, Andy. So, to bring everyone up to speed, here is where we stand this month.
Good morning, everyone. Let me give you a quick update.
I usually open with the cash flow, then move to operations.
I run operations and I lead a small team here.
That is a good question. Let me check that and get back to you.
Just to bring everyone up to speed, here is where we stand.

Hi Izabel, it's Mark from the supplier side. I'm just leaving you a quick update before Thursday. We usually ship the components in two weeks, but this order is a little bigger, so it normally takes about three. I always send you the tracking numbers by email, and they should reach you tomorrow. One more thing: we've streamlined our order process, so from now on you can approve everything in one place. If you have any questions, you know where to find me. Talk soon, and have a good week.

Good morning, team. Just a short briefing before the weekly call. As you know, Izabel runs the Thursday meeting, and she usually opens with the cash flow and then the operations update. This week, please make sure your numbers are ready by Wednesday evening. I oversee the reporting side, so I ensure that every figure is correct before it goes to the board. We often get questions from the new partners, so keep your summaries clear and short. Thank you, everyone, and let's make this a strong meeting.
